wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the FreelanceXchain platform. Explain its purpose as a decentralized freelance marketplace combining blockchain security with AI-powered skill matching. Describe the core value proposition: secure escrow payments, on-chain reputation, privacy-preserving KYC, and intelligent project-freelancer matching. Document the high-level architecture including backend services, Supabase database, and Ethereum-based smart contracts. Illustrate key user workflows such as project posting, proposal submission, contract execution, milestone-based payments, and dispute resolution. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions. Provide real-world use cases and business benefits for both freelancers and employers. Make the content accessible to technical and non-technical stakeholders alike, using clear language and practical examples from the platform's design.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the User model in the FreelanceXchain platform. Detail all field definitions including id, email, role (freelancer/employer), wallet_address, and timestamps, specifying data types and constraints in both TypeScript and PostgreSQL. Explain the role-based access control mechanism and how it influences permissions across the system. Document the relationship between User and related models such as EmployerProfile, FreelancerProfile, Project, Proposal, and Contract. Illustrate primary key configuration, unique constraints on email and wallet_address, and indexes for query optimization. Describe how the UserRepository implements CRUD operations and integrates with Supabase Row Level Security. Include a sample user record for both freelancer and employer roles, and explain validation rules enforced at both application and database levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Authentication Service. Thoroughly explain its implementation of JWT-based authentication, role-based access control (RBAC), and integration with Supabase Auth. Document key methods such as user registration, login, token refresh, and password reset. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how auth-service interacts with auth-middleware for request validation and user authorization. Explain the service's dependency on Supabase configuration and its role in managing user sessions. Address common issues like token expiration, session invalidation, and secure token storage. Provide guidance on extending the service for social logins or multi-factor authentication. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers regarding security best practices and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication middleware in FreelanceXchain. Explain how it intercepts incoming requests to verify JWT tokens using the jsonwebtoken library, extracts user identity and role information, and attaches it to the request object for downstream services. Document the integration with Supabase authentication and role-based access control (RBAC) enforcement for routes requiring specific permissions (e.g., freelancer, employer, admin). Include code examples showing token validation logic, error handling for expired or malformed tokens, and route protection patterns. Address common issues such as token refresh mechanisms, CORS compatibility, and securing sensitive endpoints. Discuss performance considerations around token parsing and caching strategies, as well as security best practices like token expiration and secure storage recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI client service in FreelanceXchain. Thoroughly explain its implementation as the core communication layer with the Google Gemini API, including request construction, retry logic with exponential backoff, and timeout handling. Document the prompt engineering strategy with templates for skill matching, skill extraction, and skill gap analysis, showing how variables are injected. Detail the response parsing mechanism that handles both raw text and JSON outputs, including markdown code block stripping. Explain the fallback mechanisms using keyword-based matching when the AI service is unavailable. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how generateContent, analyzeSkillMatch, and extractSkills functions are used. Address common issues like API rate limiting, authentication failures, and malformed responses, with solutions implemented in the retry logic and error handling. Provide performance optimization tips such as caching AI responses and managing concurrent requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for all authentication endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements (JWT) for user registration, login, token refresh, OAuth integration, and password recovery. Use Swagger/OpenAPI specifications from auth-routes.ts to generate accurate request and response models. Include detailed examples for common operations like user registration with wallet address, OAuth login flow with Google/GitHub, and password reset. Document rate limiting policies applied via rate-limiter middleware. Provide client implementation examples in JavaScript/TypeScript showing how to handle authentication headers and parse responses. Address security considerations for password storage, token expiration, and brute force protection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Escrow System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the FreelanceEscrow smart contract and its integration layer. Explain the contract's role in securely holding funds during freelance engagements, including deposit, release, and refund mechanisms. Detail the milestone-based payment flow, fund locking conditions, and withdrawal validation logic. Document the TypeScript escrow-contract service that interfaces with the contract using ethers.js, covering transaction construction, gas estimation, and event listening for PaymentReleased and FundsDeposited events. Explain how the payment-service triggers escrow operations and handles blockchain confirmations. Include security considerations such as reentrancy protection, withdrawal authorization, and timeout safeguards. Provide examples of interacting with the escrow system from backend services and describe testing strategies for escrow workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Tables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for all tables in the FreelanceXchain Supabase PostgreSQL database. Detail each table's purpose, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships as defined in schema.sql. Cover the users, projects, proposals, contracts, disputes, notifications, skills, skill_categories, freelancer_profiles, employer_profiles, kyc_verifications, reviews, messages, and payments tables. Explain primary keys, foreign key relationships, and CHECK constraints for data integrity. Document the use of UUIDs for primary keys and timestamptz for audit fields. Include examples of how these tables interact in real-world scenarios such as project creation, proposal submission, and contract execution. Reference the TABLES constant in supabase.ts to show how table names are accessed programmatically in the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the user registration endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the POST /api/auth/register endpoint with request body schema including email, password, role (freelancer/employer), optional name, and optional Ethereum wallet address. Detail validation rules such as email format, password strength (min 8 characters), wallet address pattern (0x followed by 40 hex characters), and role enumeration. Explain the 201 success response with AuthResult schema containing user data, accessToken, and refreshToken, as well as error responses for 400 (validation errors) and 409 (duplicate email). Include examples of valid registration payloads with wallet integration and demonstrate how the authRateLimiter middleware protects against abuse. Document the interaction between auth-routes.ts and registerWithSupabase function in auth-service.ts, and explain how Supabase handles initial OAuth user creation before role assignment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the project creation endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Detail the POST /api/projects endpoint, including required fields such as title (minimum 5 characters), description (minimum 20 characters), requiredSkills (array of skill objects with valid UUIDs), budget (minimum 100), and deadline. Explain the authentication requirement using JWT and role-based access control for employers only. Document the 201 Created response with the full Project object, and error responses including 400 for validation failures and 401 for unauthorized access. Provide a complete curl example showing how to create a project with skills and milestones. Include validation rules and constraints enforced by the service layer and middleware.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication security system in FreelanceXchain. Detail JWT-based authentication flow including access token (1-hour expiry) and refresh token (7-day expiry) management. Explain the Bearer token format validation in auth-middleware.ts and error handling for missing or malformed tokens. Document token validation logic, expiration checks, and payload structure (user ID, role, wallet address). Include integration with Supabase authentication and secure token storage recommendations. Provide examples of successful and failed authentication flows, including error responses like AUTH_MISSING_TOKEN, AUTH_INVALID_FORMAT, and AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED. Address security best practices such as token revocation, secure transmission over HTTPS, and protection against token leakage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Proposal Submission
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the proposal submission endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Detail the POST /api/proposals endpoint, including HTTP method, URL pattern, request body schema with required fields (projectId, coverLetter, proposedRate, estimatedDuration), and authentication via JWT token. Explain the role-based access control that restricts submission to users with 'freelancer' role. Document validation rules: coverLetter must be at least 10 characters, proposedRate must be a positive number (≥1), estimatedDuration must be at least 1 day. Include the response schema based on the Proposal model and describe possible status codes: 201 on success, 400 for validation errors, 401 for unauthorized access, 404 if project not found, and 409 for duplicate proposals. Provide a real-world example of submitting a proposal for a web development project with a cover letter, $50/hour rate, and 14-day estimated duration. Explain how the service layer interacts with the database through the proposal repository and triggers relevant notifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dispute Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the dispute creation endpoint POST /api/disputes. Document the required request body fields (contractId, milestoneId, reason), authentication via JWT, and role-based access control allowing any contract party to initiate a dispute. Explain the validation rules for UUIDs and non-empty reason strings. Detail the response schema including the full Dispute object with status 'open' and empty evidence array. Include a practical example of creating a dispute with a milestone delivery issue. Describe the backend flow from route to dispute-service and integration with the DisputeResolution smart contract. Address common errors such as 409 Conflict when a dispute already exists for the milestone and 404 Not Found for invalid contract/milestone IDs. Provide guidance on client implementation and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Milestone Completion
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the milestone completion endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the POST /api/payments/milestones/{milestoneId}/complete route, including HTTP method, URL pattern, path parameter (milestoneId), and required query parameter (contractId). Explain that this endpoint is used by freelancers to mark a milestone as complete, which triggers a notification to the employer. Detail the authentication requirement using JWT via authMiddleware and UUID validation through validateUUID. Describe the request flow: user authentication check, contractId validation, service invocation via requestMilestoneCompletion, and response handling. Include the 200 success response schema (MilestoneCompletionResult) with milestoneId, status=submitted, and notificationSent flag. Document error responses for 400 (missing contractId, invalid UUID), 401 (unauthenticated), 403 (unauthorized), and 404 (not found). Provide a practical example of how a freelancer would call this endpoint after completing work. Explain the integration with the payment-service and how it updates the milestone status in the database via payment-repository.

wiki_catalogs.name: Submit Rating
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the rating submission endpoint in the FreelanceXchain reputation system. Detail the POST /api/reputation/rate endpoint, including required JWT authentication, request body schema (contractId, rating: 1-5, comment), and validation rules. Explain the business logic flow: authentication check, contract completion verification, eligibility to rate (only parties in the contract), and prevention of duplicate ratings. Document integration with the FreelanceReputation.sol smart contract via the reputation-service to store ratings immutably on-chain. Include a code example showing a freelancer submitting a 5-star rating with comment after contract completion. Address error responses for unauthorized access, invalid contract state, and blockchain transaction failures. Provide guidance on handling transaction confirmation and user feedback in client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Retrieve Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the GET /api/notifications endpoint that retrieves a user's notification list. Document the HTTP GET method, query parameters for pagination (continuation token, limit), and response format including notification ID, type (proposal_received, milestone_submitted, payment_released, dispute_opened, etc.), read status, creation timestamp, and relevant metadata (project title, sender name). Include a detailed example request with JWT authentication and a sample paginated response. Explain how the continuation token enables efficient cursor-based pagination to handle large notification volumes. Describe the integration between the route handler, NotificationService, and database layer. Provide guidance on client-side implementation for infinite scroll loading and error handling for expired tokens or invalid pagination tokens.

wiki_catalogs.name: KYC Submission API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for KYC submission endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the POST /api/kyc/submit endpoint including HTTP method, URL pattern, request body schema (KycSubmissionInput), and authentication requirements (JWT). Detail all required fields for international KYC: personal information (firstName, lastName, dateOfBirth, nationality), address (InternationalAddress schema), and identity document (KycDocument schema). Explain the validation rules for each field and provide example payloads for submitting a complete KYC application. Document the response codes (201 Created, 400 Validation Error, 409 Conflict) and their triggering conditions. Include practical examples showing how to properly format the request with address and document data. Address privacy considerations for transmitting sensitive personal information and explain the data encryption and storage practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Recommendations API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the project recommendations endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/matching/projects endpoint, including its authentication requirement (JWT), query parameter 'limit' with validation rules (1-50, default 10), and response schema containing ProjectRecommendation objects with projectId, matchScore, matchedSkills, missingSkills, and reasoning fields. Include a detailed example request showing how to retrieve top 15 project recommendations for a freelancer, and an example response with sample data. Explain the AI matching logic that compares freelancer skills against project requirements, calculates match scores, and identifies skill gaps. Document error responses for unauthorized access (401) and missing freelancer profile (404). Provide client implementation guidance for integrating recommendations into a dashboard UI with loading states and pagination.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Search API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the project search endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/search/projects endpoint with its HTTP method, URL pattern, and JWT authentication requirement. Detail all query parameters including keyword (for title/description search), skills (comma-separated skill IDs), minBudget, maxBudget, pageSize (1-100, default 20), and continuationToken for pagination. Provide the full request and response schema, including the ProjectSearchResult structure with items array and SearchResultMetadata (pageSize, hasMore, continuationToken). Include practical examples for searching projects by keyword 'web development', filtering by skill IDs '1,5,9', and budget range between 500-5000. Explain the server-side validation logic for numeric parameters and how invalid inputs return 400 errors. Document the integration with the search-service's searchProjects function and the underlying database indexing strategy on project titles, descriptions, and required skills. Address performance considerations for large datasets and recommend client-side implementation patterns for progressive loading.

wiki_catalogs.name: OAuth Provider Initiation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the OAuth provider initiation endpoint in FreelanceXchain. Document the GET /api/auth/oauth/:provider endpoint that redirects users to Google, GitHub, Azure, or LinkedIn for authentication. Explain the validation of provider parameters, the generation of OAuth URLs via getOAuthUrl in auth-service.ts, and the redirection mechanism. Detail the configuration of redirect URLs, PKCE flow setup, and state management for security. Include examples of client-side initiation for each supported provider and explain how the system handles invalid provider requests. Document error responses for unsupported providers and server-side failures during URL generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Users Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the users table in the FreelanceXchain Supabase PostgreSQL database. Detail each column including id (UUID primary key), email (unique, not null), password_hash (not null), role (with CHECK constraint for 'freelancer', 'employer', 'admin'), wallet_address, name, and audit timestamps. Explain the purpose of this table as the central identity store for all platform users. Document the relationship with role-specific profiles (freelancer_profiles and employer_profiles) via foreign key references. Include examples of how user roles affect access control and data visibility. Reference the TABLES.USERS constant in supabase.ts to show how this table is accessed programmatically. Address security considerations such as password hashing and RLS policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the FreelanceXchain technology stack. Detail the primary technologies: TypeScript for backend development, Express.js for REST API handling, Supabase for PostgreSQL database and authentication, Hardhat for Ethereum smart contract development, and ethers.js for blockchain interaction. Document the role of each dependency listed in package.json, categorizing them into core frameworks, blockchain tools, security packages, testing libraries, and development utilities. Explain the rationale behind technology choices, including benefits of TypeScript's type safety, Supabase's real-time capabilities and Row Level Security, and Hardhat's local blockchain testing. Describe the Docker containerization strategy and multi-stage build process. Include version compatibility requirements and third-party service integrations such as Google Gemini API for AI matching. Provide guidance on dependency management and upgrade strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Project model in the FreelanceXchain platform. Detail fields such as title, description, budget, required_skills, milestones, status, and owner_id, specifying their data types and constraints in both TypeScript and PostgreSQL. Explain how the project model supports milestone-based delivery and skill-based matching. Document the foreign key relationship to the User model (owner), and associations with Proposal and Contract models. Describe indexes on status and required_skills for efficient search and filtering. Explain how the ProjectRepository handles data access and enforces business rules like ownership validation and status transitions. Include a sample project record with defined milestones and required skills, and detail validation logic for budget ranges and milestone configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Project Service. Explain its implementation of project lifecycle management including creation, updating, retrieval, and deletion of freelance projects. Document core methods such as createProject, updateProject, getProjectById, and searchProjects, detailing their parameters, return types, and error handling. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how the service validates project data, enforces business rules (e.g., required skills, milestone definitions), and coordinates with the matching-service for AI-powered recommendations. Illustrate its interaction with the project-repository for data persistence and relationship with proposal-service for bid management. Address common issues like invalid project state transitions, permission checks, and data consistency. Provide performance considerations for large-scale project searches and indexing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the validation middleware in FreelanceXchain. Thoroughly explain its implementation using schema validation libraries (e.g., Joi or Zod) to validate incoming request payloads, query parameters, and URL parameters against defined contracts. Document how it ensures data integrity before reaching business logic by rejecting malformed or invalid inputs with appropriate HTTP error responses. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing validation schemas for user registration, project creation, and proposal submission. Explain the middleware's error aggregation strategy and formatting of validation errors for client consumption. Address integration with Express.js, performance implications of synchronous validation, and extensibility for custom validation rules. Provide guidance on defining reusable validation schemas and handling edge cases like optional fields and nested objects.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Assistant
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI assistant service in FreelanceXchain. Explain its role in enhancing user productivity through AI-generated content for proposals, project descriptions, and dispute analysis. Document the three main functions: generateProposal for creating personalized cover letters, generateProjectDescription for helping employers create detailed project posts, and analyzeDispute for providing objective dispute resolution recommendations. Detail how each function retrieves contextual data from repositories (project, freelancer profile, dispute) to create comprehensive prompts for the AI client. Include examples of the prompt templates used for each use case, showing how project details, freelancer skills, and evidence are incorporated. Explain the response parsing and validation process that ensures structured JSON output. Address error handling for cases like missing profiles or AI service unavailability. Provide guidance on how to extend the assistant with new capabilities by adding additional prompt templates and analysis functions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for all project management endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements (JWT) for creating, updating, and retrieving projects. Include detailed examples for project creation with required skills and budget, milestone configuration, and project search with filters. Document query parameters for filtering by skills, budget range, and keyword search. Explain the project status lifecycle (draft, open, in_progress, completed, cancelled) and constraints on modifications after proposal acceptance. Provide client implementation examples showing how to create a project with milestones and retrieve project proposals. Address validation rules for project title, description, budget, and deadline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reputation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the on-chain reputation system implemented in FreelanceReputation.sol. Explain how reputation scores are calculated, updated, and stored for freelancers and employers based on completed projects and peer reviews. Document the contract's functions for submitting ratings, computing weighted scores, and preventing manipulation through cooldown periods and validation rules. Describe the reputation-contract service that handles blockchain interactions, including transaction signing for reputation updates and event listening for ReputationUpdated events. Explain integration with the reputation-service for off-chain caching and score aggregation. Include data model details for reputation records, performance considerations for read-heavy workloads, and strategies for handling reputation disputes. Provide usage examples and security practices for maintaining score integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the indexing strategy implemented in the FreelanceXchain database for query optimization. Detail all indexes created in schema.sql including idx_users_email, indexes on foreign keys for profiles, projects, proposals, contracts, disputes, notifications, kyc_verifications, skills, reviews, messages, and payments. Explain the performance benefits of these indexes on common query patterns such as finding projects by employer, retrieving proposals for a specific project, or fetching notifications for a user. Discuss the trade-offs between read performance improvement and write performance overhead. Provide guidance on when additional indexes might be needed based on query patterns and how to monitor index effectiveness using PostgreSQL tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Login
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the user login endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the POST /api/auth/login endpoint with LoginInput schema requiring email and password. Detail the authentication flow including credential validation, JWT token generation, and response format with AuthResult containing user data, accessToken, and refreshToken. Explain error responses for 400 (validation errors) and 401 (invalid credentials), including proper error code handling. Include examples of successful login requests and responses, and demonstrate how the authRateLimiter middleware prevents brute force attacks. Document the integration between auth-routes.ts and the login function in auth-service.ts, and explain how the system validates credentials against Supabase authentication while maintaining application-specific user data and roles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Retrieval
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for project retrieval endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Cover both GET /api/projects for listing open projects with filtering capabilities and GET /api/projects/{id} for retrieving a specific project by UUID. Document query parameters including keyword search, skills (comma-separated skill IDs), minBudget, maxBudget, limit, and continuationToken for pagination. Explain the response structure containing items array, hasMore boolean, and continuationToken. For single project retrieval, detail the 200 success response with Project schema and 404 response when project is not found. Include examples of searching for projects by JavaScript skill and $500-1000 budget range.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Based Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the role-based access control (RBAC) system in FreelanceXchain. Explain the three-tier role model: Freelancer (access to proposals, contracts, profile), Employer (project creation, hiring, payments), and Admin (full system access). Describe how user roles are extracted from JWT tokens during authentication and enforced across API routes. Document route-level authorization checks and middleware integration. Provide examples of permitted and restricted operations for each role, such as freelancers being unable to create projects or employers lacking dispute resolution privileges. Include guidance on role assignment during user registration and potential escalation paths. Address common issues like privilege misalignment and token tampering detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Proposal Retrieval
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the proposal retrieval endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document two GET endpoints: /api/proposals/{id} for retrieving a specific proposal by UUID, and /api/proposals/freelancer/me for fetching all proposals submitted by the authenticated freelancer. For each endpoint, specify HTTP method, URL pattern, path parameters (where applicable), authentication requirements (JWT), and response schema based on the Proposal model. Explain access controls: any authenticated user can view a specific proposal if they have access, while only freelancers can access their own proposal list. Detail error responses including 400 for invalid UUID format and 404 when proposal is not found. Provide usage examples: an employer viewing a detailed proposal and a freelancer checking their submission history. Include information about how the service layer validates ownership and permissions before returning data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Evidence Submission
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the evidence submission endpoint POST /api/disputes/{disputeId}/evidence. Document the path parameter (disputeId), authentication requirements (JWT), and request body schema with type (text, file, link) and content fields. Explain that any party involved in the dispute can submit evidence while the dispute status is 'open' or 'under_review'. Detail the response that returns the updated Dispute object with appended evidence. Provide examples for each evidence type, including text descriptions, file upload references, and external link citations. Describe the validation rules and error responses including 403 Forbidden if the user is not a dispute participant and 400 Bad Request if the dispute is already resolved. Explain the integration between the backend service and the blockchain-based evidence logging in the DisputeResolution contract. Include client-side implementation tips for handling different evidence types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Milestone Approval
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the milestone approval endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the POST /api/payments/milestones/{milestoneId}/approve route, including HTTP method, URL pattern, path parameter (milestoneId), and required query parameter (contractId). Explain that this endpoint is used by employers to approve a completed milestone, triggering payment release from the blockchain escrow. Detail the authentication requirement using JWT and UUID validation. Describe the request flow: user authentication, contractId validation, service invocation via approveMilestone, and response handling. Include the 200 success response schema (MilestoneApprovalResult) with milestoneId, status=approved, paymentReleased flag, transactionHash, and contractCompleted indicator. Document error responses for 400, 401, 403, and 404. Provide a practical example of an employer approving a milestone and receiving the blockchain transaction hash. Explain the integration between payment-service and escrow-contract for executing the blockchain transaction to release funds, and how the status is updated in the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Get Reputation Score
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop API documentation for retrieving a user's reputation score in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/reputation/score/:userId endpoint, including optional time-range parameters and JWT authentication requirements. Explain how the service aggregates on-chain ratings from the FreelanceReputation.sol contract to calculate an average score with weighted recent ratings. Detail the response schema including overall score, total reviews, and breakdown by rating value. Include a practical example of fetching a freelancer's reputation score to display in a profile view. Describe caching strategies in the reputation-service to optimize performance and reduce blockchain reads. Address edge cases such as users with no ratings and handle them gracefully in the response. Provide client-side implementation tips for displaying dynamic reputation indicators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mark Notification as Read
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read endpoint that marks a specific notification as read. Document the HTTP PATCH method, path parameter for notification ID, and empty request body. Specify the 204 No Content success response and error responses for unauthorized access (401), not found (404), or invalid ID format (400). Include a practical example showing how to update the read status after a user views a proposal acceptance notification. Explain the backend flow from route to NotificationService to database update, emphasizing JWT-based ownership verification via auth-middleware. Address idempotency considerations and potential race conditions in high-frequency scenarios. Provide best practices for client-side state synchronization after marking notifications as read.

wiki_catalogs.name: KYC Liveness Verification API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the face liveness verification endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the three related endpoints: POST /api/kyc/liveness/session to create a new liveness session, GET /api/kyc/liveness/session to retrieve the current session, and POST /api/kyc/liveness/verify to submit verification results. For each endpoint, specify the HTTP method, URL pattern, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements (JWT). Explain the liveness challenge types (blink, smile, turn_left, turn_right, nod, open_mouth) and how they are randomized in each session. Detail the required request parameters for verification, including sessionId, capturedFrames (base64 encoded images), and challengeResults. Provide example flows showing how a client should guide a user through the liveness check process. Document the LivenessCheck response schema and status values (pending, passed, failed, expired). Include error handling guidance for common issues like session expiration and failed challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Freelancer Recommendations API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the freelancer recommendations endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/matching/freelancers/{projectId} endpoint, including its authentication requirement (JWT), path parameter 'projectId' (UUID format), query parameter 'limit' with validation rules (1-50, default 10), and response schema containing FreelancerRecommendation objects with freelancerId, matchScore, reputationScore, combinedScore, matchedSkills, and reasoning fields. Include a detailed example request showing how to retrieve top 20 freelancers for a specific project, and an example response with sample data. Explain the AI matching logic that combines skill match scores with blockchain-verified reputation scores to generate a weighted ranking. Document error responses for invalid UUID (400), unauthorized access (401), and missing project (404). Provide client implementation guidance for displaying recommendations in a project management interface with filtering and contact options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Freelancer Search API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the freelancer search endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/search/freelancers endpoint with its HTTP method, URL pattern, and JWT authentication requirement. Detail all query parameters including keyword (for bio search), skills (comma-separated skill IDs), pageSize (1-100, default 20), and continuationToken for pagination. Provide the full request and response schema, including the FreelancerSearchResult structure with items array and SearchResultMetadata (pageSize, hasMore, continuationToken). Include practical examples for searching freelancers by keyword 'React expert', filtering by skill IDs '3,7', and combining both filters. Explain the server-side validation logic for pageSize and how invalid inputs return 400 errors. Document the integration with the search-service's searchFreelancers function and the underlying database indexing strategy on freelancer bios and skills. Address performance considerations for text search on user profiles and recommend client-side implementation patterns for filter combinations and infinite scrolling.

wiki_catalogs.name: OAuth Callback Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the OAuth callback handling system in FreelanceXchain. Cover both the GET /api/auth/callback endpoint for PKCE flows and the POST /api/auth/oauth/callback endpoint for implicit flows. Explain how the system processes authorization codes, exchanges them for sessions using exchangeCodeForSession, and handles token extraction from URL fragments. wiki_catalogs.name: Payment API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for all payment processing endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements (JWT) for milestone completion, approval, dispute, and payment status. Include detailed examples for marking a milestone as complete, approving a milestone to release payment, disputing a milestone, and checking contract payment status. Document query parameters for contractId and milestoneId. Explain the payment flow from milestone completion to approval and the conditions under which disputes can be created. Provide client implementation examples showing how to complete a milestone and check payment status. Address the integration between API endpoints and blockchain transactions for payment release.

wiki_catalogs.name: Milestone Registry
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the MilestoneRegistry.sol contract responsible for tracking project progress and triggering payments. Explain how milestones are defined, approved, and marked as completed within the contract, including validation rules for sequential completion and approval requirements. Document the event-driven architecture with MilestoneCompleted and MilestoneApproved events consumed by backend services. Describe the milestone-registry service implementation using ethers.js for reading milestone states and submitting completion transactions. Explain integration with the contract-service for synchronizing on-chain milestone status with off-chain contract records. Cover data consistency strategies, error handling for failed transactions, and performance optimization for frequent status checks. Include examples of milestone workflows and monitoring practices for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Recovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the password recovery functionality in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the requestPasswordReset and updatePassword flows, including endpoints for initiating recovery and completing the password change. Explain the email-based verification process and token expiration policies. Detail the security measures in place to prevent abuse, including rate limiting and token invalidation after use. Document the implementation in auth-service.ts and how it integrates with Supabase's authentication system while maintaining application-specific user data and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Proposal Listing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for retrieving proposals for a specific project in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/projects/{id}/proposals endpoint that returns all proposals submitted for a given project. Explain that this endpoint requires employer role authentication and verifies the requesting user owns the project. Detail the pagination parameters (limit and continuationToken) and response structure containing proposals array, hasMore flag, and continuationToken. Include the 403 Forbidden response when attempting to access proposals for a project owned by another employer. Provide an example response showing multiple proposals with freelancer details, proposed amounts, and timelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Smart Contract Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for smart contract security patterns in FreelanceXchain. Explain reentrancy guards implemented in FreelanceEscrow.sol to prevent recursive call attacks during fund withdrawals. Document access control modifiers like onlyEmployer, onlyFreelancer, and onlyAdmin used across contracts for privilege enforcement. Describe input validation within contracts to prevent malformed data processing and arithmetic overflows. Cover secure transaction patterns including checks-effects-interactions and proper error handling via require statements. Detail the use of pull-over-push payment pattern in escrow to minimize fund exposure. Include security considerations for contract upgrades, ownership management, and interaction with external oracles. Reference testing strategies for contract vulnerabilities using Hardhat.

wiki_catalogs.name: Proposal Withdrawal
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the proposal withdrawal endpoint in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the POST /api/proposals/{id}/withdraw endpoint that allows freelancers to withdraw their pending proposals. Detail the HTTP method, URL pattern with proposal ID parameter, authentication via JWT, and role restriction to 'freelancer' users. Explain the state transition: only proposals in 'pending' status can be withdrawn, updating their status to 'withdrawn'. Describe the response schema which returns the updated Proposal object and list HTTP status codes: 200 on success, 400 for invalid state or format, 401 for unauthorized access, 403 for permission violations, and 404 if proposal is not found. Provide a use case where a freelancer withdraws a proposal after accepting another project. Include information about validation logic that prevents withdrawal of already accepted, rejected, or previously withdrawn proposals, and ensures the freelancer owns the proposal.

wiki_catalogs.name: KYC Data Retrieval API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for KYC data retrieval endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the GET /api/kyc/status endpoint that returns the current user's complete KYC verification record, including personal information, document details, liveness check results, and verification status. Also document the country-specific endpoints: GET /api/kyc/countries for retrieving all supported countries, and GET /api/kyc/countries/{countryCode} for getting requirements for a specific country. Specify the HTTP methods, URL patterns, authentication requirements (JWT for status endpoint), and response schemas for each endpoint. Explain the KycVerification response schema with its status lifecycle (pending, submitted, under_review, approved, rejected) and tier levels (basic, standard, enhanced). Provide example responses for both successful retrievals and error cases (404 Not Found). Include guidance on how clients should use the country requirements data to dynamically build KYC forms based on user location.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the employer_profiles table in the FreelanceXchain Supabase PostgreSQL database. Detail each column including id (UUID primary key), user_id (unique foreign key to users), company_name, description, industry, and audit timestamps. Explain the purpose of this table as the organizational identity for employers posting projects. Document its one-to-one relationship with the users table and its role in building trust through verified company information. Include examples of how employer profiles are displayed when browsing projects. Reference the TABLES.EMPLOYER_PROFILES constant in supabase.ts and the idx_employer_profiles_user_id index. Address RLS policies and considerations for public versus private profile data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware & Interceptors
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the middleware layer in FreelanceXchain's Express.js application. Explain the role of each middleware component in the request processing pipeline. Detail the authentication middleware's JWT verification and role-based access control, validation middleware's request schema checking using libraries like Joi or Zod, error-handling middleware's centralized exception processing, request-logger's audit trail generation, security-middleware's implementation of Helmet.js protections, and rate-limiter's abuse prevention using Redis or memory stores. Document the execution order of middleware in various routes and how they contribute to security, reliability, and observability. Provide examples of custom middleware creation and integration. Include performance implications of middleware chaining and best practices for error propagation through the middleware stack.

wiki_catalogs.name: KYC Verification Model

Explain how this model enables privacy-preserving identity verification through blockchain-anchored document hashes. Document the foreign key relationship to the User model and unique constraint per user. Describe indexes on verification_status for compliance reporting and user_id for quick lookups. Explain how the KYCRepository interacts with the KYCVerification smart contract to verify status. Include a sample kyc record with document hash and detail validation rules for document types and status transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dispute Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Dispute Service. Document core methods such as createDispute, submitEvidence, resolveDispute, and getDisputeHistory, detailing their interaction with the DisputeResolution smart contract via dispute-registry. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how the service validates dispute eligibility, manages evidence lifecycles, and coordinates with payment-service for fund redistribution. Illustrate its integration with notification-service for stakeholder updates and contract-service for contract suspension. Address common issues like evidence tampering prevention, time-bound resolution windows, and arbitration logic implementation. Provide guidance on extending the service for automated mediation or third-party arbitrator integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rate Limiting Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the rate limiting middleware in FreelanceXchain. Explain its implementation for preventing abuse and denial-of-service attacks by limiting the number of requests a client can make within a specified time window. Document the use of in-memory stores or Redis for tracking request counts, IP-based or token-based identification, and configurable thresholds for different endpoints (e.g., higher limits for authenticated users). Include examples of integration with Express.js using libraries like express-rate-limit, handling rate limit exceeded responses with appropriate HTTP 429 status codes, and retry-after headers. Address distributed deployment challenges, synchronization across instances, and monitoring for anomalous traffic patterns. Discuss configuration best practices, such as distinguishing public vs. private endpoints and protecting authentication routes from brute-force attacks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dispute API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for all dispute resolution endpoints in the FreelanceXchain system. Document the dispute status lifecycle (open, under_review, resolved) and role-based access controls (any party can create, only admins can resolve). Provide client implementation examples showing how to create a dispute and submit evidence. Address validation rules for dispute reasons and evidence types, and explain the dispute resolution process and outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contract Agreement
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the ContractAgreement.sol smart contract that formalizes freelance engagements on-chain. Describe secure handling of document images with temporary storage and access controls. Cover encryption strategies for sensitive fields in the database and secure transmission via TLS. Include audit logging for KYC access and monitoring for unauthorized data queries.

Document its critical role in tracking fund flows through the FreelanceEscrow.sol contract. Include examples of how payment status changes reflect blockchain confirmations and how tx_hash enables blockchain exploration. Reference the TABLES.PAYMENTS constant in supabase.ts and indexes on contract_id, payer_id, and payee_id for performance. Address RLS policies ensuring financial privacy.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# FreelanceXchain: Decentralized Freelance Marketplace Project Overview

## 1. Project Introduction

FreelanceXchain is a decentralized freelance marketplace platform that combines blockchain technology with AI-powered skill matching to create a secure, transparent, and efficient ecosystem for freelancers and employers. The platform leverages smart contracts for payment escrow, dispute resolution, and reputation management, while incorporating AI to enhance the matching process between talent and projects.

**Core Goals and Objectives:**
- Create a trustless environment for freelance transactions using blockchain technology
- Implement secure KYC verification with privacy-preserving on-chain storage
- Enable automated payment processing through smart contract-based escrow systems
- Build a transparent reputation system that records feedback on-chain
- Facilitate AI-driven skill matching between freelancers and projects

**Target Audience:**
- Freelancers seeking secure payment protection and reputation building
- Employers looking for verified talent with transparent credentials
- Administrators managing the platform and verifying user identities
- Developers interested in blockchain-based marketplace applications

## 2. Technical Architecture

The FreelanceXchain platform follows a modular backend architecture with clear separation of concerns, combining traditional REST API patterns with blockchain integration layers.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client] --> B[API Layer]
    B --> C[Business Logic]
    C --> D[Data Access]
    D --> E[(Supabase Database)]
    C --> F[Blockchain Integration]
    F --> G[(Ethereum/Sepolia)]
    F --> H[Smart Contracts]
```

The architecture consists of several key layers:
- **API Layer**: Express.js server with comprehensive middleware for security, logging, and error handling
- **Business Logic**: Service layer implementing core marketplace functionality
- **Data Access**: Repository pattern for database operations using Supabase
- **Blockchain Integration**: Dedicated services for interacting with smart contracts
- **External Services**: AI matching, wallet integration, and identity verification

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend] --> B[API Gateway]
    B --> C[Authentication]
    B --> D[User Management]
    B --> E[Project Management]
    B --> F[Contract System]
    B --> G[Payment Processing]
    B --> H[Dispute Resolution]
    C --> I[Supabase Auth]
    D --> J[Supabase DB]
    E --> J
    F --> K[Smart Contracts]
    G --> K
    H --> K
    K --> L[Ethereum Network]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **src/app.ts**: Application initialization and middleware configuration
- **src/index.ts**: Server startup and dependency injection
- **hardhat.config.cjs**: Blockchain deployment configuration

### Core Modules
- **Smart Contracts**: Solidity implementations for escrow, reputation, and KYC
- **Service Layer**: Business logic encapsulation in the services directory
- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction with type safety
- **Blockchain Client**: Abstraction layer for blockchain interactions

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: Managed through dotenv with type-safe configuration
- **Supabase Integration**: Database schema and authentication configuration
- **Swagger Documentation**: API specification and interactive documentation
- **Hardhat Configuration**: Blockchain network settings and deployment scripts

### External Dependencies
- **Blockchain**: Hardhat, ethers.js, and Web3 integration
- **Database**: Supabase for PostgreSQL storage and authentication
- **Security**: Helmet, CORS, JWT authentication, and rate limiting
- **Development**: TypeScript, ESLint, Jest for testing, and Swagger for API docs

### Integration Points
- **Blockchain Networks**: Sepolia testnet and local Hardhat network
- **Wallet Integration**: Ethereum wallet address management
- **AI Matching**: AI-powered skill matching service
- **Identity Verification**: KYC compliance with on-chain verification records

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[API Routes] --> B[Services]
    B --> C[Repositories]
    C --> D[Supabase]
    B --> E[Blockchain Client]
    E --> F[Smart Contracts]
    E --> G[Ethereum Network]
    B --> H[AI Assistant]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Decentralized Escrow System
The platform implements a smart contract-based escrow system that securely holds payments until project milestones are completed. Funds are released automatically when conditions are met, reducing payment disputes.

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ContractCreated
    ContractCreated --> FundsDeposited: Employer deposits funds
    FundsDeposited --> MilestoneInProgress: Milestone starts
    MilestoneInProgress --> MilestoneCompleted: Freelancer completes work
    MilestoneCompleted --> PaymentReleased: Automatic payment release
    MilestoneInProgress --> DisputeOpened: Disagreement on completion
    DisputeOpened --> DisputeResolved: Admin/DAO resolution
    DisputeResolved --> PaymentReleased: Final payment decision
    PaymentReleased --> [*]
```

### On-Chain Reputation System
A transparent reputation system records user ratings on the blockchain, creating an immutable history of performance that follows users across the platform.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    User->>API: Submit rating after contract completion
    API->>ReputationService: Process rating submission
    ReputationService->>BlockchainClient: Create transaction
    BlockchainClient->>ReputationContract: Submit rating to blockchain
    ReputationContract-->>BlockchainClient: Transaction confirmation
    BlockchainClient-->>ReputationService: Confirmation receipt
    ReputationService-->>API: Update local records
    API-->>User: Confirmation of rating submission
```

### Privacy-Preserving KYC Verification
The KYC system verifies user identities while preserving privacy by storing only cryptographic hashes of sensitive data on-chain, with full documentation in the database.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Submits KYC Data] --> B[Server Validates Data]
    B --> C[Generate Cryptographic Hash]
    C --> D[Store Hash on Blockchain]
    D --> E[Store Encrypted Data in Database]
    E --> F[Admin Verification Process]
    F --> G[Approve/Reject on Blockchain]
    G --> H[Update User Status]
```

### AI-Powered Skill Matching
An AI assistant analyzes project requirements and freelancer profiles to suggest optimal matches, improving the efficiency of the hiring process.

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AIAssistant {
        +matchSkills(project, freelancers)
        +analyzeProfile(profile)
        +generateRecommendations()
    }
    
    class Project {
        +title: string
        +description: string
        +requiredSkills: string[]
    }
    
    class FreelancerProfile {
        +skills: string[]
        +experience: Experience[]
        +rating: number
    }
    
    AIAssistant --> Project
    AIAssistant --> FreelancerProfile
    AIAssistant --> SkillService
```

- [kyc-contract.ts](/media/protech/New Volume/Projects/Supabase/FreelanceXchain/src/services/kyc-contract.ts)
- [reputation-contract.ts](/media/protech/New Volume/Projects/Supabase/FreelanceXchain/src/services/reputation-contract.ts)
</blog>
